CHAPTER XXII. GOOD name -is rather to be chosen than great riches, and Joving favour rather than silver and gold.

What Have You Done with My Name? By Elder Mervyn B. Arnold Of the Seventy Someday each one of us will have to account to our Savior, Jesus Christ, for what we have done with His name. When President George Albert Smith was young, his deceased grandfather George A. Smith appeared to him in a dream and asked, “I would like to know what you have done with my name.” President Smith responded, “I have…

4 Onmy son! cleave to wise men who fear God and be like them, and go not near the ignorant, lest thou become like him and learn his ways.
Therefore you have no temple, you have no place to learn his ways.
4 Onmy son! cleave to wise men who fear God and be like them, and go not near the ignorant, lest thou become like him and learn his ways.

R. Jochanan (and according to others, R. Elazar), said: "The wife of a man does not die [frequently] unless he is requested to pay [his donation] and has not to pay, as it is said (Prov. 22, 27) If thou have nothing to pay, why should he take away thy bed from under thee?" R. Jochanan said again: "To him whose first wife dies, it is as if the Temple had been destroyed in his days, as it is said…
